hero_013.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize the Passive shield and the long-range artillery of Skill 2 to clear minions and harass the enemy from a safe distance. Since you gain a shield every time you cast a skill, you can stably progress through the laning phase while minimizing damage taken. The knockback from the enhanced basic attack is also useful for poking.
hero_013.midGame: In the mid game, use the stun effect of Skill 2 and the continuous damage from its field to assist teammates in getting kills or poke during objective fights. The combo of repositioning with Skill 1's dash, landing an enhanced basic attack, and following up with Skill 2 is powerful. You need to maintain your shield to increase your sustained combat capability.
hero_013.lateGame: In the late game, with full equipment, the magic damage and stun from Skill 2 become a massive threat to enemies. Before teamfights, poke from a long distance to whittle down the enemy's HP. If you can land Skill 2 on the enemy carry and stun them, you can create an advantageous situation instantly. Do not step forward carelessly; strictly maintain positioning that leverages your range.
hero_013.teamfight: In teamfights, disrupt the enemy formation with the long-range stun from Skill 2. When enemies approach your team, deploy the energy barrier of your Ultimate to stun enemies in a wide area. If you dash into the middle of the enemy team with Skill 1 and use your Ultimate, you can lock down multiple enemies for a long time, creating an environment where your team can easily deal damage.
hero_013.commonMistakes: A common mistake is overestimating your durability and diving alone into the enemy team, missing your Ultimate, and taking focus fire. Also, if the accuracy of your Skill 2 is low, your contribution to the team drops significantly. Even with the Passive shield, you are still a mage, so you must be careful when your skills are on cooldown and you are defenseless.
hero_013.strengths: The greatest strength is that all skills have hard CC (knockback, stun). Skill 2 in particular can safely stun from a long distance, and thanks to the Passive shield, you have high durability despite being a mage. It has an excellent balance of offense, defense, and CC, making it highly capable of supporting allies.
hero_013.weaknesses: The weakness is that Skill 2 travels in a straight line and is easy to dodge once the projectile speed is learned. Also, while deploying the Ultimate, you cannot move, making you vulnerable to being interrupted by long-range attacks or CC. Furthermore, due to high reliance on magic power, damage tends to be somewhat lacking until fully equipped, and missing skills leaves a large opening.

hero_014.earlyGame: In the early game, use the extended range from Skill 1's roll and the enhanced basic attack to pressure enemies from a safe distance. Hitting basic attacks reduces the cooldown of Skill 1, so keep attacking minions to increase Skill 1's uptime. The movement speed increases after rolling, so strictly practice hit-and-run tactics.
hero_014.midGame: In the mid game, initiate your burst damage with the Skill 2 bomb. When Skill 2 hits, the enemy's physical defense is reduced, and basic attacks against marked enemies deal additional damage. The combo of "Skill 1 -> Skill 2 -> enhanced basic attack" can shred HP instantly. In small skirmishes in the jungle or river, utilize your mobility to reposition.
hero_014.lateGame: In the late game, she can output extremely high physical damage. In teamfights, position yourself in the backline, adjusting your position with Skill 1 while reliably whittling down the enemy front line with enhanced basic attacks. The long-range ballista of her Ultimate is perfect for finishing off fleeing enemies or dealing AoE damage to multiple enemies as poke before a fight starts.
hero_014.teamfight: In teamfights, it is crucial to keep an appropriate distance with Skill 1's roll to avoid ever being caught by enemy assassins or fighters. Lower enemy defense from behind your frontline with Skill 2, and spam enhanced basic attacks to deal damage. Utilize the Passive effect that increases movement speed when enemies are nearby, and keep attacking while constantly moving.
hero_014.commonMistakes: A common mistake is rolling forward into the enemy team with Skill 1 and getting bursted down instantly. Use the roll for escaping or repositioning, and only roll aggressively when safety is guaranteed. Also, missing Skill 2 significantly drops your damage output, so if you lack the awareness to land it reliably instead of just throwing it randomly, you won't be effective.
hero_014.strengths: Her greatest strengths are overwhelming burst damage, and the high mobility and long range provided by Skill 1. The defense reduction effect from Skill 2 allows her to deal heavy damage even to tanky enemies, and the long-range AoE attack from her Ultimate is also powerful. Weaving in basic attacks increases skill uptime, making her sustained damage excellent as well.
hero_014.weaknesses: Her weakness is the lack of CC skills and extremely low durability; once caught, she will be killed without being able to do anything. Also, since her main damage source relies on basic attacks and Skill 1, she is vulnerable to attack speed reduction and blind effects, and losing mobility puts her at a massive disadvantage instantly. A positioning mistake leads to instant death.

hero_015.earlyGame: In the early game, focus on stacking Skill 2 while using the periodically triggering Passive "Dream Realm" to safely lane. Since it provides CC immunity, damage reduction, and increased movement speed, it is easy to evade enemy ganks. Use the illusionary butterflies of Skill 1 from a distance to slow enemies and support your allies.
hero_015.midGame: From the mid game onwards, stick with your teammates and use the movement speed buff and damage stacks from Skill 2 to gain an advantage in small skirmishes. Skill 2 grants stacks to you and your allies, providing a powerful movement speed buff at max 5 stacks, making it extremely strong for chasing or retreating. Keep the automatic trigger of the Passive in mind when positioning.
hero_015.lateGame: In the late game, protecting allies with your Ultimate against the enemy's powerful CC skills becomes the most important task. As you are the cornerstone of teamfights, always position yourself near your allied carries. Restrict enemy movements with the wide AoE slow of Skill 1, boost ally mobility with Skill 2, and control the board with the CC immunity from your Passive and Ultimate.
hero_015.teamfight: In teamfights, your primary role is to deploy the "Dream Realm" Ultimate the moment the enemy uses crucial CC skills, cleansing CC for all allies and granting them a shield and CC immunity. At the same time, stand on the frontline and spam Skill 2 to deal damage to enemies while boosting your team's mobility. Utilize your own CC immunity to disrupt the enemy formation.
hero_015.commonMistakes: Common mistakes include using the Ultimate solely to protect yourself, or wasting it before the enemy's CC arrives. It is the strongest defensive skill for protecting the entire team, so you must accurately judge the timing. Also, despite having CC cleanse from the Passive, your base durability isn't exceptionally high, so be careful not to get isolated.
hero_015.strengths: The greatest strength is the periodic self-CC cleanse and damage reduction, along with the massive AoE CC cleanse and shield provided to the entire team by the Ultimate. He can completely neutralize enemy control heroes. Furthermore, the stacking damage and movement speed increase from Skill 2 allow him to powerfully support allies and deal damage in extended teamfights.
hero_015.weaknesses: Lacking hard CC, his ability to proactively catch enemies or set up kills is low. He relies heavily on the environment his allies create to deal damage, and if his team isn't fed, it's difficult for him to carry the game alone. Also, his counter-measures against burst damage are limited aside from the shield, making him vulnerable to being burst down instantly.

hero_016.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize the shield and movement speed boost from Skill 1 to aggressively approach the enemy and apply pressure with the knock-up from your enhanced basic attack. Also, take advantage of your skills damaging towers and healing you at the same time; push the minion wave and hit the enemy tower to chip its health while maintaining your own HP.
hero_016.midGame: In the mid game, securely secure kills in coordination with allies using the powerful CC chain combo of Skill 1 and Skill 2. The basic flow is "approach and knock-up with Skill 1 -> stun with Skill 2". Furthermore, fully utilize the trait of being able to jam towers; forcing a tower dive to get a kill even without minions is a potent strategy.
hero_016.lateGame: In late-game teamfights and sieges, Liu Shan's tower neutralization ability shows tremendous power. Jam the enemy defense towers with Skill 1 and 2 while swiftly destroying the towers with your allies. It is crucial to land the CC of Skill 1 and 2 on the enemy carry, and use the spinning attack of the Ultimate to deal continuous damage and slow them.
hero_016.teamfight: In teamfights, stand at the very frontline, rushing into the enemy formation with Skill 1 to disrupt it. Your role is to neutralize the enemy carry by consecutively landing the knock-up from Skill 1 and the stun from Skill 2. After that, activate the spinning attack of your Ultimate to stick to enemies and continuously slow them. You can cast other skills while spinning, allowing for flexible responses.
hero_016.commonMistakes: A common mistake is forcefully chasing highly mobile enemies with Skill 1 and getting isolated from your team. Also, overestimating your tower diving capability and rushing into a tower when allies cannot follow up, resulting in taking enemy counterattacks and dying meaninglessly, is very common. Sense of distance and timing with allies are key.
hero_016.strengths: His greatest strength is his unique siege capability—he can deal direct damage to structures (towers and crystals), jam them to disable their attacks, and even heal his own HP in the process. Additionally, he possesses two types of powerful hard CC (knock-up on Skill 1 and stun on Skill 2), making him exceptionally adept at locking down enemies.
hero_016.weaknesses: His attack range is extremely short, and he cannot deal damage or apply CC unless he gets in point-blank range. If kited from a distance with slows or CC, he will be whittled down without being able to do anything. His only gap-closing tool is the movement speed boost from Skill 1, making it a weakness that enemies with mobility skills can easily escape him.

hero_017.earlyGame: In the early game, use the bouncing notes of Skill 1 to safely clear minions and harass the enemy simultaneously. After casting 3 times, his basic attack is enhanced into an energy chord that deals piercing magic damage, so smartly weave skills and basic attacks to seize control of the laning phase. Be careful about taking poke damage.
hero_017.midGame: From the mid game, participate in small skirmishes utilizing the mobility and healing effects of Skill 2. Hitting an enemy hero with Skill 2 restores HP, and taking reduced damage while moving increases your survivability. Poke with Skill 1, approach with Skill 2 at opportune moments, and focus on applying slow to assist your team in securing kills.
hero_017.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, the explosive power of the Ultimate is the key. Utilize the massive movement speed boost upon starting the performance to dive straight into the enemy formation. Since the damage reduction effect scales with the number of nearby enemies during the performance, positioning to catch as many enemies as possible is vital. Target the enemy carry and decimate them with massive AoE continuous magic damage.
hero_017.teamfight: In teamfights, following up after initiation is the rule. Wait for the enemy to use their major CC skills, then activate the Ultimate to gain acceleration and damage reduction while plunging into the center of the enemy team. Further use Skill 2 to slow enemies and heal yourself, while spamming the Passive's energy chords to unleash overwhelming AoE burst damage.
hero_017.commonMistakes: A frequent mistake is overestimating the powerful damage reduction of the Ultimate and diving first into the enemy formation without team support. No matter how tanky you become, focused fire will kill you. Also, failing to fully utilize his survivability by only hitting minions with Skill 2's heal is another common failure.
hero_017.strengths: The greatest strength is the overwhelming AoE continuous damage during the Ultimate, combined with a damage reduction effect that gets stronger the more enemies are around. He shines when diving right into the middle of multiple enemies in a teamfight, possessing the potential to single-handedly shatter the enemy formation. His sustained combat ability from the Passive's piercing attacks and Skill 2's self-heal is also excellent.
hero_017.weaknesses: His combat power and survivability without his Ultimate are extremely low, making positioning during its cooldown very difficult. Also, because he needs to get very close to enemies to deal damage, he is in danger of being killed without doing anything against long-range poke or enemies with strong hard CC (stun or knockback) that can stop his dive.

hero_018.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize your Passive, which guarantees critical strikes on attacks from behind, to quickly farm the jungle by attacking monsters from behind. Attacks from the front do not crit, so positioning is everything. When ganking a lane, use the multi-hits of Skill 1 and the dash of Skill 2 to ensure you always engage from the enemy's back.
hero_018.midGame: In the mid game, proactively target isolated or low HP enemies. Enter stealth with the Ultimate, gaining movement speed and HP regeneration as you sneak behind the enemy. Strictly execute the assassin combo: "Approach with Ultimate -> mark and gain attack speed from behind with Skill 2 -> deal damage with Skill 1" to instantly execute the enemy carry.
hero_018.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, never initiate the fight yourself. Wait for your allies to engage and the enemy's CC skills to be depleted, or when enemy HP is low, before diving in from stealth with your Ultimate. Since kills or assists reset the cooldown of all skills, run through the battlefield aiming for consecutive kills.
hero_018.teamfight: In teamfights, aim for the enemy backline (mages and marksmen) from behind. Approach unnoticed with the Ultimate, then unleash burst damage with Skill 2 and 1 for a quick kill. Once you get a kill or assist, your Passive resets your skills, allowing you to re-enter stealth with the Ultimate to either disengage or hunt the next target.
hero_018.commonMistakes: The most common mistake is fighting an enemy face-to-face. Frontal attacks do not crit, so your damage output will be zero. Another typical failure is charging in from stealth during a teamfight without waiting for your team, absorbing enemy CC, and dying instantly. Engaging when you cannot secure a kill is also a fatal error as it misses the skill reset.
hero_018.strengths: Her greatest strength is the overwhelming burst damage from guaranteed critical hits attacking from behind, combined with the full skill cooldown reset on kills and assists. If she can participate in a kill, she can repeatedly use her Ultimate's stealth and heal, making her ability to systematically wipe out the enemy team (pentakill potential) extremely high.
hero_018.weaknesses: Since she deals no damage in frontal combat, she is weak in situations where enemies don't let her get behind them, or when their backs are against a wall. Also, having very low durability and no hard CC, she will instantly melt if her stealth is broken or if she gets stunned upon diving. Her heavy reliance on teammates to whittle down enemy HP is another weakness.

hero_019.earlyGame: In the early game, trade aggressively utilizing the petrify effect from the Passive and the periodically acquired shield. Stick to the enemy with the dash from Skill 1 and the slow from the enhanced basic attack, aiming to trigger petrify while dealing damage. The ideal approach is to fight when the shield is up, creating an HP advantage and seizing lane priority.
hero_019.midGame: In the mid game, pressure enemies with the high burst damage of Skill 2. The combo of approaching with Skill 1 and landing Skill 2 is strong, especially because hitting the center of Skill 2 deals extra damage. If the Passive petrify triggers, you can reliably land the center of Skill 2, chunking down the enemy's health instantly.
hero_019.lateGame: In the late game, act as the frontline for your team's formation. Dive into the middle of the group with the spinning attack of the Ultimate, spreading damage and petrify to a wide area of enemies. While the Ultimate is active, movement speed increases and shields continuously stack, giving you high durability. Play the role of pressuring the enemy carry while acting as a shield for your allies.
hero_019.teamfight: In teamfights, dive into the enemy formation with Skill 1, instantly deploying the Ultimate to deal continuous damage and petrify the entire enemy team. Because the shield is constantly replenished while spinning, you can disrupt their formation while absorbing enemy attacks. For enemies whose CC has worn off, aim for a center hit with Skill 2 to deal massive damage and secure the kill.
hero_019.commonMistakes: Since the cast animation of Skill 2 is somewhat slow, blindly casting it on enemies who aren't petrified or slowed often results in it being easily dodged, meaning you deal no damage. Also, since petrify relies on chance (50%) and has an 8-second cooldown per target, beware of getting isolated and killed from forced dives assuming petrify will land.
hero_019.strengths: The greatest strength is the extremely powerful control capability where all damage instances can trigger hard CC (petrify), even if probability-based. The continuous AoE petrify from the Ultimate has the power to shatter teamfights. Furthermore, she is a versatile fighter who combines massive burst damage from Skill 2's center hit with high survivability from her shields.
hero_019.weaknesses: Weaknesses include the long animation of Skill 2 making it hard to land, and being unable to cast other skills while channeling the Ultimate, which is her main damage source. Also, the reliance of CC on probability introduces instability, where petrify might fail to proc at crucial moments, letting enemies escape or counterattack. Due to not having top-tier mobility, she is susceptible to being kited.

hero_020.earlyGame: In the early game, attach the Skill 1 bomb to enemy heroes to harass from a safe position. Whittle down enemy health with the explosion's magic damage and slow effect, while avoiding enemy attacks using the movement speed boost from the Passive. As a support, prioritize helping your allied carry farm and avoid unnecessary skirmishes to focus on leveling up.
hero_020.midGame: In the mid game, the time manipulation of Skill 2 becomes extremely important. By increasing the movement speed of the entire team, you can boost gank success rates or help allies escape enemy pursuit. Since a portion of the damage taken is restored at the end of the effect, timing Skill 2 just as the enemy uses their skills will drastically increase your allies' survivability.
hero_020.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, the deployment of the wide-area field from the Ultimate decides the outcome. Use it to split the enemy formation or block escape routes. The silence and massive slow upon hit disrupts enemy skill casting, creating an opening for your team to attack. You need to constantly boost and heal your allies utilizing the cooldown reduction from Skill 2.
hero_020.teamfight: In teamfights, position yourself in the backline. Activate Skill 2 in response to the enemy's initiation (engage) to absorb and restore damage taken by your allies. It is most effective to fire the Ultimate into grouped enemies to silence them and interrupt their combos. Keep throwing Skill 1 bombs relentlessly, and continuously adjust your positioning with the Passive movement speed boost.
hero_020.commonMistakes: A common mistake is wasting Skill 2 just for a simple movement speed boost, leaving it on cooldown and unable to heal allies when the enemy's powerful burst damage arrives. Also, throwing the Ultimate into a random spot where enemies easily dodge it means losing your greatest disruption tool, putting your team in crisis. Skill timing is everything.
hero_020.strengths: The movement speed buff for the entire team and the pseudo-nullification of damage (rewind healing) from Skill 2 are his greatest strengths, boasting extremely high resilience against enemy burst damage. Furthermore, the massive AoE silence and slow from the Ultimate provide powerful counter-engage capabilities to ruin enemy skill combos, excelling in teamfight control.
hero_020.weaknesses: Sun Bin himself lacks hard CC (such as stuns or knock-ups) and cannot output burst damage, making him completely dependent on the firepower and coordination of his team. When allies are not fed, his ability to turn the game around is weak. Also, because Skill 2's heal is based on "damage taken," it cannot prevent instant death damage and is weak against continuous damage.

hero_021.earlyGame: In the early game, use basic attacks and skills to apply poison marks on the enemy, harassing them with continuous damage. Control the area with the poison vial of Skill 1, while applying healing marks to yourself and your allies with Skill 2 to keep HP high. Focus on stacking up to 5 marks, and gradually build an advantage through favorable damage trades.
hero_021.midGame: From the mid game, demonstrate your strength in continuous damage and AoE healing during small skirmishes. Keep applying poison stacks to enemies while constantly spreading healing stacks to allies with Skill 2. If you cast your Ultimate when both sides have accumulated stacks, you can deal massive burst damage to enemies while significantly healing your entire team, steering the battle in your favor.
hero_021.lateGame: In the late game, with full equipment, the continuous poison damage and healing amount become massive. In teamfights, position in the backline, whittling down the enemy frontline while continuously healing allies. Stack management is everything; detonating the Ultimate the moment 5 stacks are applied to both allies and enemies allows you to achieve both overwhelming teamfight sustain and AoE burst damage.
hero_021.teamfight: In teamfights, do not push too far to the frontline. Relentlessly spam Skill 1 and 2 from behind your allies. Maintain your allies' HP and stack poison on enemies simultaneously. Once enemy marks are full or ally HP drops dangerously, detonate instantly with the Ultimate. Because chaotic fights make it easier to apply stacks to multiple targets, the longer a fight drags on, the more it becomes Bian Que's domain.
hero_021.commonMistakes: Pushing too far forward trying to get a kill, drawing focus fire, and easily dying due to low durability is a common mistake. Additionally, panicking and casting the Ultimate when only 1-2 stacks are accumulated results in mediocre damage and healing, completely failing to utilize Bian Que's strengths. Poor stack management is fatal.
hero_021.strengths: His unique ability to simultaneously deal continuous poison damage to enemies and provide AoE healing to allies is his greatest strength. Detonating the Ultimate at 5 stacks delivers massive AoE damage and healing, possessing the potential to completely turn the tide of a teamfight. The longer the battle lasts, the wider the gap between poison and healing becomes, overwhelming the enemy.
hero_021.weaknesses: With extremely low mobility and lacking any movement skills for escape or hard CC for self-defense, he has no way to protect himself if an assassin gets close. Furthermore, because he requires "time" to accumulate stacks before dealing burst damage or healing, he cannot react to ultra-high burst damage ambushes that end fights instantly, often dying before he can act.

hero_022.earlyGame: In the early game, utilize the AoE damage and self-healing from the scythe of Skill 1 to lane tenaciously. Taking damage triggers a passive increase to movement speed and healing effects, and grants a chance to auto-cast Skill 1, making him very strong in melee brawls. Use the charge attack to simultaneously clear minions and harass the enemy.
hero_022.midGame: In the mid game, use the pull of Skill 2 to coordinate with ally ganks or block enemy escape routes. Because Skill 2 has a slight delay before pulling back, the trick is to predict the enemy's movement and place it ahead of them. It deals extra damage to low HP targets, so you take on the role of catching and executing fleeing enemies. Proactively secure vision.
hero_022.lateGame: In the late game, act as the absolute main tank and initiator of the allied formation. Since stacking bonus HP extends the taunt duration of the Ultimate up to a maximum of 2.5 seconds, it is crucial to properly build defensive items. Dive the enemy carry with the Ultimate and lock them down with a long taunt, creating the time and environment for your allies to deal maximum damage.
hero_022.teamfight: The initiation of a teamfight rests entirely on Bai Qi's Ultimate. With shadow power charged, jumping into a clustered group of enemies with the Ultimate to taunt multiple targets is his most important job. Immediately after taunting, charge Skill 1 to heal while dealing damage, place Skill 2 to pull back fleeing enemies, and annihilate the opposing team alongside your allies.
hero_022.commonMistakes: A common mistake is diving alone with the Ultimate from a distance where allies cannot follow up. Even if you land the taunt, lacking allied fire support means you will just get surrounded and killed. Also, since the Ultimate's taunt duration relies on bonus HP, building offensive items instead of defense leaves you failing your role and becoming just a squishy target.
hero_022.strengths: His greatest strength is possessing one of the best lockdown skills in the game—a wide AoE and long-duration "taunt" from his Ultimate. By forcing enemies to attack him, he can completely disable enemy carries from casting skills. Additionally, his durability in the dead center of the enemy team is incredible, thanks to the passive procs from taking damage and the healing from Skill 1.
hero_022.weaknesses: The weaknesses are the difficulty of landing Skill 2 due to its delay, and the lack of any immediate gap-closer other than the Ultimate. If you miss the Ultimate, you easily become a sitting duck, and long-range heroes adept at kiting will whittle down your health before you can even get close. Also, in the early stages when bonus HP is low, the taunt duration is short.

hero_023.earlyGame: In the early game, command Skill 1 and Skill 2 to summon shadow crows and maintain shadow power during the laning phase. Linking an enemy hero with the Skill 2 chain drains their attack power while dealing continuous damage and healing you, making it exceptionally advantageous for 1v1 damage trades. Always keep crows deployed to speed up minion clearing.
hero_023.midGame: In the mid game, leverage your overwhelming 1v1 prowess to actively split push. If a single enemy comes to defend, link Skill 2 to win the duel; if surrounded by multiple enemies, use the teleportation of Skill 1 or the invincibility of the Ultimate to disengage safely. Invade the enemy jungle and draw the enemy's attention.
hero_023.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, rather than engaging frontally, it's effective to flank or attack from the rear aiming for the enemy carry. Approach with Skill 1, land Skill 2 on the enemy mage or marksman to absorb their damage output. If in danger, disengage using the invincibility and movement speed boost of the Ultimate, then re-enter the fight persistently leveraging the passive healing.
hero_023.teamfight: In teamfights, the top priority is linking the Skill 2 chain to the enemy hero with the highest damage output. Since it absorbs physical and magic attack, it greatly diminishes the enemy's firepower. Maintain the maximum number of shadow crows (6) to scatter continuous damage and healing, and if focused, use the Ultimate to negate damage and make the enemy waste their skills.
hero_023.commonMistakes: Missing the Skill 2 chain prevents you from absorbing attack power and healing yourself, drastically reducing combat capability. Forcing a duel while the chain is missed is the biggest mistake. Also, using the Ultimate's invincibility offensively can leave you isolated in the enemy formation with no escape, leading to death, so be very careful about when to use the invulnerability.
hero_023.strengths: Thanks to the attack power absorption of Skill 2 and the astonishing self-healing of the passive shadow crows, she boasts top-tier performance in 1v1 brawls. Furthermore, she possesses extremely high survivability with the full invincibility and debuff cleanse of the Ultimate, paired with the long-range mobility of Skill 1. Her elusive playstyle of drawing enemy aggro and then escaping is her strength.
hero_023.weaknesses: Having absolutely no hard CC (stuns or knock-ups), she struggles to lock down enemies to protect allies or securely catch fleeing targets. Also, lacking burst damage and relying on sustained damage, she cannot out-heal incoming instantaneous ultra-burst damage and will die. Being overly specialized in small-scale skirmishes rather than large teamfights is her weakness.

hero_024.earlyGame: In the early game, the top priority is hitting an enemy hero with Skill 1 to put your weapon into an enchanted state. Basic attacks after enchanting deal true damage and restore HP, making him extremely strong in lane brawls. Absorb souls from minions or enemies with Skill 2 to gain a shield, minimizing damage taken while aggressively taking damage trades.
hero_024.midGame: In the mid game, dominate small skirmishes utilizing the true damage of the enchanted state and the durability from the Skill 2 shield. Land Skill 1 on clustered enemies to deal massive damage to multiple targets while healing. Since the shield can be converted to HP, you can fight tenaciously. Bolster your combat power with items that provide attack speed and physical defense.
hero_024.lateGame: Late-game teamfights are Lu Bu's domain. Dive right into the center of the enemy formation with the massive AoE descent of the Ultimate, knocking up multiple enemies. Upon landing, immediately use Skill 1 to enter the enchanted state, gaining the defensive buff of the killing zone, and spam true damage basic attacks. He wields overwhelming destructive power that can instantly slice through even enemy tanks.
hero_024.teamfight: In teamfights, the timing of the Ultimate is everything. When enemies are grouped or in sync with ally CC, dive in with the Ultimate to shatter the enemy formation. After landing, you MUST hit an enemy hero with Skill 1 to become enchanted. Staying inside the zone increases defense and slows enemies touching the edge, so rampaging within the zone is the basic tactic.
hero_024.commonMistakes: The most fatal mistake is missing Skill 1, losing the enchanted state, and continuing to brawl with normal basic attacks. Your damage output plummets, and since you cannot heal, you will die easily. Also, because the Ultimate's animation is long, aiming it where highly mobile enemies can easily dodge it, leaving you isolated under focused fire, is another common failure.
hero_024.strengths: The greatest strengths are the "defense-ignoring true damage" and "HP restoration on every basic attack" granted by the enchanted state, allowing him to easily cut down even the tankiest of enemies. Furthermore, the ultra-wide engage and formation-splitting capability of his Ultimate are among the best in the game, giving him the terrifying carry potential to single-handedly flip a teamfight from a disadvantage.
hero_024.weaknesses: His movements are generally sluggish, and the long cast animations of Skill 1 and the Ultimate make it easy for highly mobile enemies to dodge his attacks. Additionally, if he cannot maintain the enchanted state (by missing Skill 1), he absolutely cannot unleash his true potential. Missing the Ultimate also leaves a huge opening, making him vulnerable to kiting from a distance.
